title: Dire-cat
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Cat}} {{Species |image=[[File:Dire-cat.jpg]] |name=Dire-cat |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]]{{GalaxiesAED}} |class=[[Feline/Legends|Feline]], [[Mammal/Legends|Mammal]] |subspecies=Ferine razorfang |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |haircolor=Gold |feathers= |eyecolor=Red |distinctions=Shoulder spikes |lifespan= |origin=*[[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] *[[Talus/Legends|Talus]] |habitat=Grassland |diet=[[Diet/Legends|Carnivore]]{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=item|Corellian Carnivore Meat}} |language= }} {{Quote|Unh... You think this cut is going to ''kill'' me?"
"No... But ''they'' will.|Tor Vizsla and Jango Fett, right before the former is eaten by dire-cats|[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]]}} '''Dire-cats''' were carnivorous [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Alien/Legends|creatures]] that lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. They had spines coming out of their backs and shoulder blades, and would attack animals that had the scent of [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. Their keen sense of smell was also able to pick up traces of contaminants, such as synthetic [[Poison/Legends|poison]], and as such avoid tainted meat. During [[42 BBY/Legends|42 BBY]], a pack of dire-cats attacked, [[Death/Legends|killed]], and ate the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian warrior]] [[Tor Vizsla]] after his stomach had been cut open with a switch-blade, heavily exposing the scent of his blood to the nearby [[Cat/Legends|cats]]. Dire-cats could also be found on the planet of [[Talus/Legends|Talus]], existing as a [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]] known as the ferine razorfang. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Why didn't they kill you, too?"
"I guess they thought I was bad meat.|A Corellian child wonders why Jango Fett, who was poisoned, wasn't killed by dire-cats|}} Dire-cats were [[Feline/Legends|feline]] [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]] who lived on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. Carnivores, dire-cats had an acute sense of [[Nose/Legends|smell]] that allowed them to pick up and track the scent of [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. However, if the blood was contaminated or unclean, such as [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] blood, dire-cats could detect this and avoid tainted meat. A full-grown dire-cat was fairly large, muscular [[Alien/Legends|creature]], capable of downing a grown [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] of nearly two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] with little difficulty. Dire-cats were covered in [[Color/Legends|golden]], bristly [[Hair/Legends|fur]], had red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and the skin of their noses were colored black. Dire-cats walked on four legs, each leg ending in a large padded [[Limb/Legends|paw]]. Front paws each had five digits, each tipped in a sharp gray claw. Their hind legs ended in paws with only three digits, however. Another notable aspect of dire-cats was that the backs of their shoulders were covered in sharp pointed spines that stuck out from beneath their fur. Adults had larger, more prominent spines than the juveniles. Although the task was difficult, dire-cats could be tamed. ==History== {{Dialogue|Jailer Knash|One more chirp from you, little bird, and you'll regret it.|Vette|Chirp. Chirp, chirp.|Knash|Hrmph. I warned you.|Vette|Ow! Jerk. If you don't like that, just say so. I can do other animals, too. Dire-cat, frog-dog, Kowakian monkey-lizard, you name it.|attr=Knash and Vette|src={{TORcite|type=class|Sith Warrior|Judge and Executioner|Korriban}}}} Dire-cats were primarily found on Corellia, a planet located in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]], and had been in recorded history as early as the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3643 BBY]]. During an event known as the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], which spanned from [[3653 BBY]] to [[3642 BBY]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] named [[Vette]] made a claim to a jailer named [[Knash]] that she could imitate the sound that a dire-cat could make. Much later, during [[42 BBY/Legends|42 BBY]], a family of the cats, a dominant adult and three cubs, followed behind the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] [[Tor Vizsla]] and his rival [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] as they dueled each other across the terrain of Corellia after a narrow escape from a detonating [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. After falling into a river and washing up on a lake bed, Vizsla injected Fett with poison. Before Vizsla could deal the killing blow, Fett slashed Vizsla's stomach open with a switch-blade, causing the warrior to bleed profusely. The blood that had been spilled was just enough to attract the family of dire-cats, who attacked, [[Death/Legends|killed]], and ate Vizsla. After the cats had eaten their fill, two of the cubs inspected Fett's unconscious form, but sniffing out his poisoned blood, they backed away and left Fett to rest in the grass. The dire-cats were gone by the time Fett regained consciousness. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] who often visited Corellia would sometimes come into contact with dire-cats and occasionally hunt them. Spacers also encountered ferine razorfangs when visiting Talus. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]'' {{Mo}} *{{GalaxiesAED}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided Quick Reference Guide]]'' *[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Total Experience'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{SWGWiki|Dire Cat}} {{Interlang |pt=Gato selvagem |pl=Pantera piaskowa}} [[Category:Carnivorous creatures]] [[Category:Cats]] [[Category:Creatures of Corellia]] [[Category:Non-sentient species]] [[Category:Predatory creatures]]
